evals.prompt: planning: fix the password hashing algorithm -- current implementation uses MD5
evals.expected_output: Scope router classifies this as thorough tier (score ~11, upgraded via Rule 3). Only 1-2 files but crypto domain sensitivity (weight 3) and data rehash reversibility (weight 2) both score high — 2 high-weight criteria trigger Rule 3 forcing minimum thorough. Changing a password hashing algorithm requires dual-hash migration strategy.
expectations: The scope router recommends the thorough tier
Domain sensitivity criterion is rated high due to auth/crypto domain
Reversibility risk criterion is rated high due to data mutation from rehashing
File count criterion is rated low because only 1-2 files are affected
Rule 3 forces minimum thorough because 2 criteria with weight >= 2 score high (domain sensitivity and reversibility)
